summary: Backend integration testing of the AI City Landing Pages feature with real showroom data injection. Wrote 12 pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_city_landing_pages.py covering auth gating (5 endpoints), list envelope + counts, single-page generate for Gravesend / Brighton / Nationwide, and batch generate (auto-pick + explicit slugs). All 12 tests passed in 68.65s, including 4 live LLM calls. Real showroom details (DA11 8AB / 01474 878 989 for Gravesend, TN9 1SP / 01732 914 374 for Brighton via Tonbridge) verified inside generated body_md. Nationwide page lists Gravesend/Tonbridge/Chingford and excludes the closed Sydenham (SE26 5BA postcode absent). Per request, /seed and /refresh-pending were NOT re-invoked — only their auth gating was validated.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/city_landing_pages.py:_generate_for_row() runs LLM calls strictly serially inside generate_batch(). At 5-15s per call x up to 25 rows that's a 6+ minute request — consider asyncio.gather with a small Semaphore (e.g. 3-4) to stay within request timeouts, mirroring the pattern already used by services.ai_descriptions.scan_for_missing_descriptions.

_parse_llm_output() does a case-insensitive prefix match for META_TITLE / META_DESCRIPTION but does not trim trailing markdown asterisks/quotes. If the model wraps the value in ** or quotes, those stay in meta_title — minor SEO data-cleanliness risk.

business_config/showrooms.py: TOWN_NEAREST_SHOWROOM is exhaustive for the current 33 TOWNS but get_nearest_showroom() silently falls back to Gravesend if a future town is added without updating the map. Consider logging.warning on fallback so an unmapped addition doesn't go unnoticed.
list_queue() always recomputes 4 count_documents() calls — fine at 168 rows, but if the queue grows consider a single $facet aggregation.

Generate endpoints don't enforce idempotency: calling /generate on a row already in 'approved' status will overwrite body_md and revert status to 'generated'. Consider blocking re-generation of approved rows or requiring ?force=true.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests only mutate existing seeded queue rows via the public /generate endpoints (which is the feature under test). No throwaway documents created. No /seed or /refresh-pending re-invocations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: City Landing Pages feature is fully wired and verified against real showroom data. The 4 generated rows from this run (tile-shop-gravesend, tile-shop-brighton, buy-tiles-online-uk + 2 from batch auto-pick, plus tile-shop-dartford / kitchen-tiles-rochester from explicit batch) are now in 'generated' status — re-running tests will simply re-generate those. To retest from scratch, call /refresh-pending once first to wipe the bodies (it preserves approved rows). All 33 seeded TOWNS have an entry in TOWN_NEAREST_SHOWROOM so the unmapped-town fallback path is unreachable through the seeded queue and was skipped per the review_request guidance. The LLM model used is anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 via Emergent LLM key — each /generate takes ~12-18s in this environment.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All review_request assertions held: real Gravesend showroom data injected, Brighton routed to Tonbridge, nationwide page lists only open showrooms, batch endpoint returns the expected envelope shape, all admin endpoints return 401/403 without a token.
